title: Adithyapuram Sun Temple
tagline: Adithyapuram Sun Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Adithyapuram Surya Temple is a Hindu temple located in Iravimangalam near Kaduthuruthy in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Adithyapuram Surya Temple is a Hindu temple located in Iravimangalam near Kaduthuruthy in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is noted mainly as the only 'Adithya' (Hindu solar deity) shrine in the state. The temple is situated just 200 m (0.20 km) from the main highway leading to Vaikom. It is about 3 km from Kaduthuruthy, 17 km from Ettumanoor and 16 km from Vaikom.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Neendoor
topPlaces.description: Neendoor is a very small town in the Kottayam district in Kerala, India. The town is located 8 km north of Kottayam town, and is bordered by the towns of Kaipuzha, Athirampuzha, Kallara, Manjoor, Vechoor, and Onamthuruthu.

topPlaces.name: Neendoor Subrahmanya Swami Temple
topPlaces.description: Neendoor Subrahmanya Swami Temple is an ancient lord Murugan temple located in the Neendoor, Kottayam district (Kerala, India). The Neendoor Subrahanya Swami Temple is a historic site which has brought glory and fame to the local area.

topPlaces.name: Kaipuzha
topPlaces.description: Kaipuzha is a small village in Kottayam District and sub-district in the Indian state of Kerala. As of the 2001 census, it had a population of 6,991 in 1,565 households.

topPlaces.name: Manjoor (village)
topPlaces.description: Manjoor is a village located in the northern part of the Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. It lies approximately 20 kilometers north of the district capital, Kottayam, and is situated along the Kottayam–Vaikom bus route.

topPlaces.name: Pandavam
topPlaces.description: Pandavam () is a village in Aymanam panchayath in Kottayam District in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located near Kudayampady on Kottayam - Olassa - Parippu route.

topPlaces.name: Sreekandamangalam
topPlaces.description: Sreekandamangalam ("The City of Shiva") is a hamlet which belongs to the township of Athirampuzha in Kottayam district, Kerala, India. Sreekandamangalam is ward 22 of Athirampuzha gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Athirampuzha
topPlaces.description: Athirampuzha is a town in the Kottayam district of the Kerala state of India. It is famous as the Mahatma Gandhi University is situated here.

topPlaces.name: Pattithanam
topPlaces.description: Pattithanam is a village near Ettumanoor in the Kottayam District of Kerala, India. The name 'Pattithanam' is believed to be given by the Portuguese traders, who were looking for an ideal spot to build a church (St.Boniface Church).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Adithyapuram Sun Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Adithyapuram Sun Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Adithyapuram Sun Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (40 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Adithyapuram Sun Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Adithyapuram Sun Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Adithyapuram Sun Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Adithyapuram Sun Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Adithyapuram Sun Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Neendoor, Neendoor Subrahmanya Swami Temple, Kaipuzha, Manjoor (village), Pandavam.
